title: The finite weighted P0 average commutes with scalar multiplication

source.citation: S. C. Brenner and L. R. Scott, The Mathematical Theory of Finite Element Methods, 3rd ed., Springer, 2008, Ch. 4.
source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: The finite weighted P0 average is homogeneous with respect to scalar multiplication of the cell-value function.
assumptions: u is a cell value function
informal.expected_conclusion: The weighted average of c times u is c times the weighted average of u.

declarations.name: weightedP0Average
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Average {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) : Real := (∑ i ∈ s, m i * u i) / (∑ i ∈ s, m i)

declarations.name: weightedP0Residual
declarations.statement: def weightedP0Residual {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (i : ι) : Real := u i - weightedP0Average s m u

declarations.name: weightedP0Average_smul
declarations.statement: theorem weightedP0Average_smul {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (c : Real) : weightedP0Average s m (fun i => c * u i) = c * weightedP0Average s m u

tasks.input: Translate the finite-index weighted P0 projection fact `weightedP0Average_smul` into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem weightedP0Average_smul {ι : Type} (s : Finset ι) (m u : ι → Real) (c : Real) : weightedP0Average s m (fun i => c * u i) = c * weightedP0Average s m u
Commute the scalar through the finite sum with Finset.mul_sum and normalize by ring.

tasks.input: Identify the main finite-sum or weighted-projection lemma needed for the proof.
